title: LangGraph Technology Evaluation
evaluation_context: Evaluate LangGraph for long-running AI agent workflows.

criteria.name: Problem Fit
criteria.description: Fit for long-running AI agent workflows.
criteria.rationale: Strong fit for graph-shaped, stateful workflows.

criteria.name: Technical Capability
criteria.description: Durable execution, state, and control-flow capability.
criteria.rationale: Core orchestration and persistence capabilities are well aligned.

criteria.name: Maturity & Maintenance
criteria.description: Project maintenance, release activity, and ecosystem maturity.
criteria.rationale: Adoption is plausible but production operations still require validation.

criteria.name: Operational Complexity
criteria.description: Debugging, observability, and state management burden.
criteria.rationale: Graph state and checkpointing add operational concepts teams must learn.

criteria.name: Ecosystem & Alternatives
criteria.description: Documentation, examples, integrations, and relative fit versus alternatives.
criteria.rationale: LangGraph is strong for durable workflows but not always the simplest option.

evidence_items.claim: LangGraph is designed around graph-based stateful agent orchestration with persistence concepts.
evidence_items.evidence_summary: Official documentation describes graph state, checkpoints, and long-running workflow control.
evidence_items.notes: Use live documentation during a full research run to verify current APIs.

evidence_items.claim: LangGraph has an active open-source project and ecosystem around LangChain.
evidence_items.evidence_summary: Repository and release activity should be checked during live research before final adoption.
evidence_items.notes: This deterministic example uses representative source metadata, not live repository inspection.

evidence_items.claim: Long-running workflows require explicit validation of checkpoint storage, observability, and recovery.
evidence_items.evidence_summary: Engineering evaluation should include failure injection and recovery tests before production rollout.
evidence_items.notes: Representative evidence item used to exercise consistency and risk handling.

alternatives.name: AutoGen
alternatives.description: Multi-agent conversation framework focused on agent collaboration patterns.
weaknesses: Less directly focused on durable graph workflow state
risks: May require extra infrastructure for long-running durable workflows

alternatives.name: CrewAI
weaknesses: Durability and stateful recovery need separate validation
risks: May be less suitable for complex stateful graph workflows

experiments.name: Checkpoint recovery spike
experiments.description: Run a minimal graph with checkpoint persistence and simulate interruption/recovery.
experiments.logs_summary: Not run in deterministic pipeline e2e; required for live adoption validation.
experiments.notes: This is a recommended experiment, not an executed result.

risks: Operational complexity around checkpointing and debugging must be validated.
recommendation: Run a production-like pilot before adopting broadly for long-running workflows.

executive_summary.one_sentence_verdict: LangGraph is recommended with constraints for long-running AI agent workflows.
key_reasons: Strong fit for stateful graph orchestration
Good alignment with durable workflow needs
major_risks: Need for recovery and observability validation
executive_summary.best_fit: Teams building stateful, long-running agent workflows that need explicit control flow.

technology_overview.description: LangGraph is a graph-oriented framework for building stateful agent workflows.
technology_overview.problem_addressed: Coordinating multi-step agent workflows with state, control flow, and persistence.

core_capabilities.name: Durable stateful orchestration
core_capabilities.description: Represents agent workflows as graphs with explicit state transitions.
limitations: Requires workflow and checkpoint design discipline

risk_register.name: Operational learning curve
risk_register.description: Teams must learn graph debugging, state inspection, and checkpoint recovery.
risk_register.mitigation: Run a pilot with tracing, recovery tests, and rollback criteria.

open_questions.question: Does checkpoint storage meet the target system's recovery objectives?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Recovery guarantees are central to long-running workflow adoption.
open_questions.suggested_validation: Run failure injection against the intended deployment environment.

references.title: LangGraph Persistence Documentation
references.notes: Representative official source for deterministic pipeline validation.

references.title: LangGraph Persistence Documentation Duplicate
references.notes: Intentional duplicate to verify Consistency Notes rendering.

adoption_plan.recommendation: Run a constrained production-like pilot before broad adoption.
adoption_plan.rollout_strategy: Adopt for one long-running workflow after pilot evidence is collected.
adoption_plan.decision_deadline: After pilot and recovery tests complete.
